titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Carl Mautz Collection of Peter Britt Photographs
Mautz (Carl) Collection of Peter Britt Photographs
titlestmt.author: Sarah Lerner

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

controlnote.p: This finding aid, which is compliant with the Yale EAD Best Practice Guidelines, Version 1.0, has a MARC record in Yale's ILS with the following bib number: 14848826.

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Friday November 6, 2020 at 06:27

did.unittitle: Carl Mautz collection of Peter Britt photographs

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

did.physdesc: (5 boxes)
did.abstract: 171 carte-de-visite photographs; 53 cabinet photographs; 61 tintypes, including 1 cased double portrait; 1 cased ambrotype; 20 stereographs; 10 mounted prints; 4 photographic postcards; 5 photographs; and printed material by or relating to photographer Peter Britt, circa 1850s-circa 2000. Collected by Carl Mautz. Photographs are chiefly studio portraits of men, women, and children, including Peter Britt, Amalia Grobb Britt, Amalia Britt, and Emil Britt. Stereographs in the collection depict outdoor spaces including Crater Lake, Mount Shasta, Rogue River; the Britt household; and Jacksonville, Oregon.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This collection is open for research.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: From the collection of Carl Mautz. Purchased from Michael Vinson on the Winlock William Miller, Jr. Memorial Fund and Thomas W. Streeter Fund, 2013.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into five series: I. Cased Photographs and Tintypes, circa 1850s. II. Stereographs, circa 1860s-circa 1870s. III. Cartes de Visite, circa 1870s-circa 1880s. IV. Cabinet Photographs, 1854-1898. V. Other Photographs and Material, circa 1860s-circa 2000.

bioghist.head: Peter Britt
bioghist.p: Peter Britt (1819-1905) of Obstalden, Switzerland, was a portrait painter and photographer, meteorologist, and horticulturalist. Britt was an early settler in the Rogue Valley of Oregon Territory, building a log cabin in Jacksonville in 1852. He opened P. Britt's Photograph and Daguerreotype Room, a photography studio, in Jacksonville in 1856. Following his purchase of a stereo camera in the 1860s, Britt became an outdoor and gallery photographer, taking pictures of the Oregon Coast, the North Coast of California, the Siskiyou Mountains, the Rogue River, the Klamath River, the Cascades, and Crater Lake.

Britt married Amalia Grob (1822-1871) in 1861. The couple had three children: Emil Britt (1862-1950), Arnold Britt (1864-1864), and Amalia Britt (1865-1954). Emil Britt became a photographer, working with his father beginning in 1883.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Carl Mautz Collection of Peter Britt Photographs. Yale Collection of Western Americana, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Collections are processed to a variety of levels, depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived research value, the availability of staff, competing priorities, and whether or not further accruals are expected. The library attempts to provide a basic level of preservation and access for all collections, and does more extensive processing of higher priority collections as time and resources permit. These materials have been arranged and described according to national and local standards. For more information, please refer to the Beinecke Manuscript Unit Processing Manual.

scopecontent.head: Content Description
scopecontent.p: 171 carte-de-visite photographs; 53 cabinet photographs; 61 tintypes, including 1 cased double portrait; 1 cased ambrotype; 20 stereographs; 10 mounted prints; 4 photographic postcards; 5 photographs; and printed material by or relating to photographer Peter Britt, circa 1850s-circa 2000. Collected by Carl Mautz. Photographs are chiefly studio portraits of men, women, and children, including Peter Britt, Amalia Grobb Britt, Amalia Britt, and Emil Britt. Stereographs in the collection depict outdoor spaces including Crater Lake, Mount Shasta, Rogue River; the Britt household; and Jacksonville, Oregon.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The Carl Mautz Collection of Peter Britt Photographs is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Series I is organized into two subseries: Case Photographs and Tintypes in Sleeves. Each subseries is arranged by subject.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Series I contains cased photographs and tintypes in sleeves of unidentified individuals. The number in parentheses indicates the quantity of images present.

did.unittitle: Ambrotype, sixth plate in a case, copy photograph of a studio portrait of a man of a European-American and Native-American ancestry holding a rifle, identified as a trapper

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Image is the same captured by Britt as his first wet plate negative on 1858 February 10

did.unittitle: Tintype double portrait, sixth plate in a case, a studio portrait of a man

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Series II contains stereographs chiefly of identified locations including Crater Lake, Mount Shasta, Rogue River, and Jacksonville, Oregon.

did.unittitle: "Big John and Little Richard" outside wooden house
Portrait of man reading in a garden
Wagon Road around "Mule Hill", Applegate River

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Series III is organized into two subseries: Identified Subjects and Unidentified Subjects. Each subseries is arranged alphabetically.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Series III contains carte-de-visite photographs of identified and unidentified individuals. The number in parentheses in the second subseries indicates the quantity of images present.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Series III is organized into two subseries: Identified Subjects and Unidentified Subjects. Each subseries is arranged alphabetically.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Series IV contains cabinet photographs of identified and unidentified individuals. The number in parentheses, if present, indicates the quantity of images in each file.

did.unittitle: Ennis, Reverend, wife, and four children
Prim, Charles, wife, and four children

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Series V is organized into three subseries: Mounted Prints, Prints, and Non-Photographic Materials. Each subseries is arranged alphabetically.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Series V contains mounted prints, prints, and non-photographic materials.

photograph 8 x 5 cm, in paper frame 13 x 9 cm

did.physdesc: photograph 21 x 28 cm
postcard 9 x 14 cm

did.unittitle: Newspaper advertisement for Peter Britt, Photographer
Wine tag with advertisement for P. Britt & Son
